model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: This looks like a true coin-flip, but I’d lean Virginia by a hair because the Cavaliers’ full-season predictive profile is essentially equal to Tennessee’s while coming in with the steadier résumé and better record. Both teams defend at a very similar top-15 level, so the game may come down to execution in a slower, half-court setting, which fits Virginia’s style slightly better. Tennessee absolutely has a credible path if its physicality disrupts Virginia’s offense, but with no clearly confirmed availability edge pushing this toward the Vols, Virginia is the narrow bracket pick.

reasoningStep.title: Virginia over Tennessee in a near toss-up
reasoningStep.summary: Ratings show almost no separation between these teams, with Virginia a hair ahead in both KenPom and Torvik and carrying the better overall record. Because both defenses are elite and no major confirmed injury edge surfaced, I give a slight lean to Virginia’s steadier season-long profile and likely comfort in a half-court game.
evidence: KenPom: Virginia No. 13 (AdjEM 26.49, AdjO 122.2, AdjD 95.7) vs Tennessee No. 15 (AdjEM 26.06, AdjO 121.1, AdjD 95.0).
Torvik: Virginia No. 14 (AdjOE 121.75, AdjDE 95.67) vs Tennessee No. 15 (AdjOE 121.51, AdjDE 95.48).
Records: Virginia 29-4 vs Tennessee 22-11, suggesting a steadier season-long résumé despite nearly identical efficiency quality.
Web/news searches surfaced some Tennessee injury chatter earlier in March, but no clearly confirmed, matchup-altering availability edge for either side before this pick.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-midwest-3
Current game label: Midwest Round of 32 3
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Tennessee (tennessee) vs Virginia (virginia)
Prior committed picks: 50
